summary:
The tongue develops primarily from the tuberculum impar, hypobranchial eminence, and lingual swellings—all arising from the first to third pharyngeal arches—while arytenoid swellings originate from the second pharyngeal arch and are part of laryngeal structure formation rather than tongue development. Therefore, arytenoid swellings do not contribute to tongue development.  

\boxed{D}

---

The tongue develops from structures derived from the first, second, and third pharyngeal arches—including the tuberculum impar, hypobranchial eminence, and lingual swellings—but not from arytenoid swellings. Arytenoid swellings give rise to laryngeal components such as the arytenoids and play no direct role in tongue formation.
